name: Austin (Python Frame Stack Sampler)
pricing_notes: Open source (GPL-3.0); P403n1x87 / Gabriele N. Tornetta

performance.notes: Near-zero overhead sampling profiler — no instrumentation

agent_readiness.notes: Frame stack sampler for Python — attaches to running process without instrumentation. austin -p <PID> > profile.austin → attach to running process. austin python myscript.py > profile.austin → profile from start. austin -i 100 → sample every 100 microseconds. austin -C → CPU time. Threads: austin --children. ausrun: austin python script.py | flamegraph.pl > flames.svg. FlameGraph visualization. austin2speedscope for Speedscope. austin-tui: text-mode live profiler (TUI). Async aware: captures asyncio coroutine frames. No code changes required. Multi-process: --children. Output: Austin format or collapsed stacks for flamegraph.pl. Integrates with VS Code Austin extension.

gotchas: GPL-3.0 — copyleft license may affect commercial use
Root/admin privileges sometimes needed for ptrace
Output format requires separate visualization (flamegraph.pl, speedscope)
Less well-known than py-spy or pyinstrument
best_when: Profiling running production Python processes without restart
Generating flamegraphs for async Python code
avoid_when: When py-spy covers the same need (more popular, better documented)
GPL license is incompatible with project
summary: Austin is a near-zero-overhead Python frame stack sampler. austin -p <PID> or austin python script.py → captures stacks. Flamegraph output via flamegraph.pl or Speedscope. Async-aware. Live TUI via austin-tui. Attaches to running processes. GPL-3.0 by P403n1x87. No code instrumentation needed. Root/admin sometimes required for ptrace. VS Code extension available. py-spy is more mainstream for same use case.
